description:
Per docs/SPEC-tracker-state.md SS7 (bl-40e9). Depends on the unified checkout (bl-8a9a).

- Introduce .balls/project.json on the tracker branch, project-owned: version, id_length, min_bl_version, plugins. config.json keeps the workspace-owned fields: the tracker address, target_branch, delivery, review, worktree_dir, protected_main, require_remote_*, auto_fetch_on_ready, stale_threshold_seconds.
- project.json is read through a .balls/project.json symlink into .balls/state-repo. On overlap, project.json WINS -- enforced project ownership, and the migration path (old clients fall back to built-in defaults). config.json is never symlinked.
- Plugin config (.balls/plugins/*) is project config, on the tracker branch. Plugin auth (.balls/local/plugins/) and BALLS_IDENTITY stay per-workspace, never on the tracker. bl plugin enable/disable/policy writes land in project.json.

Conformance (SPEC SS16) tests 5 and 6 must exist and fail against current code before this lands.
title: Split config: config.json (workspace) vs project.json (project)
